channel.title: Insurtech Insights
channel.description: In-depth coverage of how artificial intelligence is reshaping insurance — from claims automation to underwriting intelligence.

item.title: AI Anomaly Detection for Claims Fraud: A Practitioner’s Blueprint
item.description: Fraud costs U.S. property and casualty insurers roughly $34 billion annually—about 10% of all claims payments—per the FBI and Coalition Against Insurance...

item.title: Insurance Claims AI ROI Calculation Framework: A Practitioner’s Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: I’ve reviewed dozens of claims AI ROI models. Most overpromise by 300–500% because they ignore three critical inputs: claim leakage amortized over the policy...

item.title: How Zurich’s Canadian Operation Cut Suspected Fraud Losses by 35% Using AI
item.description: In 2021, Zurich Canada's claims team processed more than 40,000 auto and property claims. Like most insurers, they knew fraud was inflating loss ratios but had...

item.title: Predictive Analytics in Insurance Claims Settlement Optimization: A Practitioner’s Build Guide
item.description: I’ve seen claims teams drown in unstructured data—adjuster notes in PDFs, handwritten estimates, phone recordings—while trying to settle claims faster....

item.title: Why 68% of P&C underwriters still ignore 90% of their data—and how AI can fix it
item.description: I’ve watched underwriting teams drown in data for 15 years. They get 10,000 data points per risk: telematics pings, credit scores, IoT feeds, social sentiment,...

item.title: Are AI Claims Chatbots Actually Saving Insurers Money—or Just Annoying Customers?
item.description: In 2023, Lemonade’s AI claims chatbot paid out $8.2 million in claims in seconds, not days. By contrast, the industry’s average loss ratio sits at 68%, meaning...

item.title: Parametric Insurance Meets AI and Embedded IoT: A Build-Your-Own Guide
item.description: I’ve helped three MGAs launch parametric products in the last 18 months. Two flamed out because they underestimated the data plumbing; one is now writing $12 M...

item.title: Why 70% of carriers won’t have a profitable AI chatbot by 2026
item.description: Insurers spent $1.2B on AI chatbots in 2023. By 2026, that number will hit $4.1B, according to Gartner. Yet only 30% of those deployments will deliver a sub-5%...

item.title: AI Risk Assessment in Insurance: 5 Platforms Compared

item.title: Can Image Recognition AI Really Stop $40 Billion in Annual Insurance Fraud?
item.description: In 2023, the Coalition Against Insurance Fraud estimated that property & casualty insurers lost $40 billion to fraudulent claims. That’s 10% of total claims...

item.title: Building an AI Maturity Model for Insurance: A Practitioner’s Guide
item.description: I’ve reviewed dozens of AI maturity models over the years, and most of them miss the mark for insurers. They either read like academic papers with no...

item.title: Why 78% of policyholders quit a call after 90 seconds—and how voice AI keeps them on the line
item.description: It’s not a mystery: the average customer’s patience for a traditional call center call is measured in seconds, not minutes. Studies by Coveo and others show...

item.description: Insurers face a brutal choice: who builds and runs the AI systems that will decide underwriting wins, claims outcomes, and customer retention. The talent...

item.title: Can AI-driven claims processing cut your loss ratio by 15% or just move the fraud?
item.description: Insurers that have pushed AI automation past the POC stage are cutting paid loss ratios by 12–18% and slashing cycle times from weeks to sub-24 hours on simple...
